title: Amazon's Cloud Sales Growth Outpaces Investor Concerns Over AI Spending
text: Amazon reported robust first-quarter earnings on April 30, 2026, with AWS cloud revenue accelerating at its fastest pace in over three years. AWS revenue reached $37.58 billion, surpassing analyst expectations of $36.6 billion. Overall company earnings climbed to $30.3 billion, or $2.78 per share. Net sales rose 17% to $181.5 billion. AWS achieved 28% sales growth during January-March, marking the strongest quarterly performance in 15 quarters. Investors closely monitored whether Amazon's aggressive $200 billion annual capital investment would generate returns. CEO Andy Jassy stated that Amazon remained well positioned to lead, defending infrastructure investments as strategically sound. Recent high-profile deals strengthened AWS's position: expanded partnerships with OpenAI, secured $100 billion commitment from Anthropic over ten years, and signed Meta to utilize AWS infrastructure for artificial intelligence applications.
